---
title: "Projects"
---

## Graduate-Level Projects

Below are selected projects demonstrating my experience in data analysis, biostatistics, and AI-driven public health applications.

---

<div class="project-card">

<h3>LLM-Powered WASH Risk Assessment System</h3>

<p>
Access to safe water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) remains a major public health challenge, particularly in resource-limited settings. Traditional surveys often fail to capture real-time household conditions and lack adaptability.
</p>

<p>
This project develops a conversational survey system powered by large language models to dynamically collect household-level WASH risk factors. The system supports adaptive questioning, structured data extraction, response validation, and interpretable risk scoring.
</p>

<p><strong>Key Features:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Adaptive, conversation-based data collection</li>
<li>Multi-domain risk assessment framework</li>
<li>Structured outputs for downstream analysis</li>
<li>Simulation-based evaluation of consistency and robustness</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>Tools:</strong> Python, Gradio, GPT-4o, Pydantic AI</p>

<a class="button-primary" href="https://github.com/mihotelk/risk-profiler" target="_blank">View on GitHub</a>

</div>

---

<div class="project-card">

<h3>Cancer Symptom-Management RAG System</h3>

<p>
Cancer patients often face complex symptom management challenges, and access to personalized, timely information is critical for improving quality of care.
</p>

<p>
This project builds a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system to support personalized symptom-management recommendations. The system integrates patient context, symptom severity, and treatment phase to generate tailored outputs.
</p>

<p><strong>Key Features:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Multi-stage retrieval and ranking pipeline</li>
<li>Evaluation of multiple LLMs under a unified protocol</li>
<li>Focus on relevance, consistency, and usability</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>Tools:</strong> Python, RAG, MMR retrieval, LLM benchmarking</p>

<a class="button-primary" href="https://github.com/mihotelk/APE_RAG" target="_blank">View on GitHub</a>

</div>

---

<div class="project-card">

<h3>Dietary Assessment using Large Language Models</h3>

<p>
Accurately assessing dietary intake is essential for nutrition research and public health, but traditional methods are often time-consuming and prone to reporting bias.
</p>

<p>
This project evaluates whether large language models and multimodal AI systems can assess dietary intake based on user-provided text descriptions and food images. The study compares multiple models under standardized prompts.
</p>

<p><strong>Key Features:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Text-based and image-based dietary evaluation</li>
<li>Comparison across multiple LLMs and multimodal models</li>
<li>Standardized evaluation framework</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>Tools:</strong> GPT-4.1, Claude, DeepSeek, LLaVA</p>

<a class="button-primary" href="https://github.com/mihotelk/DietAI24_Eval" target="_blank">View on GitHub</a>

</div>
